posts.content:
Claude Code is terrible at UI — everyone knows it.

This repo fixes it.

It’s called awesome-design-md by VoltAgent.

→ 31 real DESIGN.md files from Stripe, Vercel, Notion, Apple, NVIDIA
→ Drop one file into your project
→ Tell your AI: “build like this”
→ It actually matches https://t.co/UW1c2Q5zp8

ai.title: Claude Code UI 개선: Stripe, Vercel, Notion 등 31개 실제 DESIGN.md 파일 활용
ai.summary: VoltAgent의 awesome-design-md 레포는 Stripe, Vercel, Notion, Apple, NVIDIA의 31개 실제 DESIGN.md 파일을 제공. 프로젝트에 파일 하나를 추가하고 AI에게 '이처럼 만들어'라고 지시하면 실제 디자인 시스템과 일치하는 UI를 생성.
ai.why_important: Claude Code의 UI 생성 약점을 실제 기업의 디자인 시스템 문서로 보완하여 실무 수준의 출력물 생성 가능.
ai.learnings: DESIGN.md 파일을 프로젝트에 포함시키면 AI가 구체적인 디자인 시스템 컨텍스트를 기반으로 일관된 UI를 생성할 수 있음.

author.name: Kevin Bertelli
author.headline: CG Supervisor - Level UP - UAI - UE Generalist - UE Paris Community Leader

posts.content:
Petit test du week-end, j'ai construit un plugin UE 5.7 qui capture le viewport (couleur + depth), envoie tout à un ComfyUI local (SDXL Turbo + LoRA Rough Pencil + ControlNet depth), et compose le résultat directement dans le viewport via un post-process material avec plusieurs modes de fusion.

Niveau performance c'est clairement pas ça, mais l'idée c'est d'avoir un sobel plus graphique. (et il y a clairement de la marge pour l'optimiser)

/
Quick weekend test — I built a UE 5.7 plugin that captures the viewport (color + depth), sends everything to a local ComfyUI (SDXL Turbo + Rough Pencil LoRA + depth ControlNet), and composites the result directly back into the viewport via a post-process material with several blend modes.
Performance is clearly not there yet, but the idea is to get a more graphic sobel. (and there's definitely room to optimize)

EDIT: For context — I don’t have a strong stance for or against generative AI, this is just an exploration.

ai.title: Plugin UE 5.7 qui capture le viewport (couleur + depth), envoie tout à un ComfyUI local
ai.summary: Construit un plugin UE 5.7 qui capture viewport (couleur + profondeur), envoie à ComfyUI local (SDXL Turbo + LoRA Rough Pencil + ControlNet depth), compose le résultat dans le viewport via post-process material avec plusieurs modes de fusion.
ai.why_important: Intégration en temps réel entre moteur 3D et pipeline ComfyUI pour stylisation graphique directe du viewport.
ai.learnings: Plugin custom UE peut envoyer color+depth à ComfyUI local et réinjecter le résultat via post-process material pour stylisation live.

author.name: Hala TEMMAMI
author.headline: I help bridge AI research and audiovisual production · Generative media

posts.content:
🎬 One image. New camera angles.

Using a ComfyUI workflow, I explored how a single photo can be reconstructed into a 3D scene to generate new viewpoints — no multi-view capture, no LiDAR.

The pipeline runs in three steps: 
→ Gaussian Splatting reconstructs the scene in 3D from a single image 
→ A new camera angle is manually set in the Gaussian Viewer 
→ Qwen generates a complete image from that new angle — filling in the missing areas that the single-image reconstruction couldn't fully capture.

This last step is key: since the 3D reconstruction is based on one image only, rotating the camera reveals incomplete regions of the scene. Qwen acts as a completion model, synthesizing the missing geometry and texture to produce a coherent final image.

Still experimental, but the implications are real — imagine generating multiple product angles from a single photo shoot.

📹 Generated with ComfyUI · Source image: 2026 Toyota Corolla Cross | Toyota 
🎓 Workflow based on Matt Hallett Visual

#AIProduction #GenerativeAI #GaussianSplatting #ComputerVision #RnD #AdvertisingTech #ComfyUI

ai.title: One image. New camera angles. Using a ComfyUI workflow with Gaussian Splatting
ai.summary: Pipeline ComfyUI en trois étapes: Gaussian Splatting reconstruit la scène 3D depuis une seule image, un nouvel angle de caméra est défini manuellement dans Gaussian Viewer, Qwen génère une image complète depuis ce nouvel angle en remplissant les zones manquantes.
ai.why_important: Permet de générer de nouveaux angles de caméra à partir d'une seule photo via reconstruction 3D et génération d'image.
ai.learnings: Combiner Gaussian Splatting (reconstruction 3D) avec Qwen (complétion d'image) permet de créer des vues arbitraires depuis une seule image source.

posts.content:
What if you could 𝗴𝗼 𝗳𝗿𝗼𝗺 𝗮 𝘀𝗶𝗻𝗴𝗹𝗲 𝗶𝗱𝗲𝗮 𝘁𝗼 𝗮 𝗳𝘂𝗹𝗹𝘆 𝗿𝗲𝗮𝗹𝗶𝘇𝗲𝗱 𝟯𝗗 𝗲𝗻𝘃𝗶𝗿𝗼𝗻𝗺𝗲𝗻𝘁 𝗶𝗻 𝗷𝘂𝘀𝘁 𝗮 𝗳𝗲𝘄 𝘀𝘁𝗲𝗽𝘀?🚀

In our latest experiment, we built a complete workflow that takes you from 𝗰𝗼𝗻𝗰𝗲𝗽𝘁 → 𝗶𝗺𝗮𝗴𝗲 → 𝟯𝟲𝟬° 𝗽𝗮𝗻𝗼𝗿𝗮𝗺𝗮 → 𝟯𝗗 𝗚𝗮𝘂𝘀𝘀𝗶𝗮𝗻 𝗦𝗽𝗹𝗮𝘁 → 𝗿𝗲𝗮𝗹-𝘁𝗶𝗺𝗲 𝗿𝗲𝗻𝗱𝗲𝗿𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗶𝗻 𝗨𝗻𝗿𝗲𝗮𝗹 𝗘𝗻𝗴𝗶𝗻𝗲.

Using tools like NanoBanana, WorldLabs, and Volinga, this pipeline shows how AI is transforming environment creation, making it faster, more iterative, and more accessible without sacrificing creative control.

💡 𝗪𝗵𝘆 𝗶𝘁 𝗺𝗮𝘁𝘁𝗲𝗿𝘀:
🔹Faster environment creation for virtual production & previs
🔹Lightweight, high-quality scenes with 3DGS
🔹Seamless integration into real-time pipelines

We break down the full workflow step-by-step, including what worked, what didn’t, and key tips for getting the best results.

👉🏻 https://lnkd.in/ggEstyAm

#VirtualProduction #AI #3DGS #GaussianSplatting #UnrealEngine #CreativeTech #Filmmaking #Volinga

ai.title: What if you could go from a single idea to a fully realized 3D environment in just a few steps?
ai.summary: Pipeline complet: concept → image → 360° panorama → 3D Gaussian Splat → rendu temps réel dans Unreal Engine. Utilise NanoBanana, WorldLabs et Volinga pour créer des environnements 3D rapidement et de manière itérative.
ai.why_important: Démontre un workflow end-to-end pour la création d'environnements 3D légers et de haute qualité via 3DGS pour production virtuelle et previs.
ai.learnings: La combinaison NanoBanana → WorldLabs → Volinga → Unreal permet de transformer un concept en environnement 3D navigable en quelques étapes automatisées.

posts.content:
Yesterday I stumbled upon Zsolt Nyírő 's work on mesh relaxations and thought it'd be a good stress test for our AI Rhino code editor.

10 minutes later I had a working physics engine inside Rhino:

• Custom Verlet integrator — no external libraries
• Particle-spring network built from any lines or polylines on a layer
• Automatic vertex welding by tolerance
• Three constraint types: pinned anchors, boundary-curve sliding, free
• Iterative Gauss-Seidel constraint solver
• Live viewport preview during the solve via a DisplayConduit
• Convergence detection

Workflow is pure Rhino: draw your springs on one layer, anchors on another, boundary curves on a third. Run. Watch it relax in real time. Bake the result.

This is essentially Kangaroo-lite, scripted from scratch in a single session.

Why this matters for architects and computational designers: the bottleneck was never the idea. It was the week of reading RhinoCommon docs between having the idea and seeing it run. When that gap collapses to minutes, the tools stop being someone else's plugin and start being things you write for the specific problem in front of you.

The shift isn't "AI writes code for you." It's that domain expertise finally compounds directly into tooling.

#computationaldesign #rhino3d #grasshopper #AEC

ai.title: Yesterday I stumbled upon mesh relaxations - 10 minutes later I had a working physics engine inside Rhino
ai.summary: En 10 minutes, a créé un moteur physique Rhino avec AI code editor: intégrateur Verlet custom, réseau particule-ressort depuis lignes/polylignes, trois types de contraintes, solveur Gauss-Seidel itératif, prévisualisation viewport live via DisplayConduit, détection de convergence.
ai.why_important: Montre la vitesse à laquelle un AI code editor peut générer des systèmes techniques complexes (moteur physique) directement intégrés dans Rhino.
ai.learnings: AI code editor peut générer un moteur physique complet avec contraintes, solveur itératif et affichage viewport en quelques minutes si le contexte est bien défini.

posts.content:
End-to-end AI workflow integrating Rhino, agent-based pipelines, and model execution directly inside the design environment.
 
Instead of isolated scripts, it’s becoming more like a system: a growing library of actions that can be triggered and composed dynamically. This is a prototype of an AI agent that can directly control Rhino and Grasshopper through natural language. Not just generating code but executing it inside the active session and responding based on results understanding its full context.
 
The current setup runs through a FastAPI + LangGraph backend, where each prompt is classified and routed depending on intent. Some requests are straightforward; others require iteration or planning. Lately I’ve been pushing it toward a ReAct-style loop:
 
run something > evaluate result > adjust > repeat
 
The goal isn’t really a chatbot, it’s integrating this into the design workflow itself. Moving away from the usual model > export > analyzes cycle, and toward something more continuous and interactive for informed design.
 
Still early and a bit rough in places but starting to feel like a different way of working. 

Also along with Burcu Gocen and Arjun Kizhakkemarakkattil Janardhanan we are starting to explore how this system could extend into sustainability and performance-driven workflows. The idea is to bring environmental metrics and targets directly into the loop, instead of running them as separate analyses. Still early, but it opens up a different way of working.

Curious if others are exploring similar directions. 
 
#AEC #ComputationalDesign #DesignAutomation #HKS #LINE #AppliedAI #DigitalTransformation #AIResearch Chad Porter

ai.title: End-to-end AI workflow integrating Rhino, agent-based pipelines, and model execution
ai.summary: Prototype AI agent that controls Rhino and Grasshopper through natural language via FastAPI + LangGraph backend. System classifies prompts, executes code inside the active session, and responds based on full context understanding.
ai.why_important: Demonstrates agent-based control of 3D tools with context-aware execution, not just code generation.
ai.learnings: Agent architecture uses FastAPI + LangGraph for routing prompts to actions that execute directly in Rhino's active session with full context.
